content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of positive integers and an integer KKK. You are allowed to perform the following operation any number of times (including zero):


choose an index jjj between 111 and NNN inclusive
choose a positive divisor ddd of AjA_jAj​ such that d≤Kd \le Kd≤K
divide AjA_jAj​ by ddd
Determine if it is possible to modify the sequence AAA in such a way that it would satisfy the following condition: there is no positive integer strictly greater than 111 which divides every element of AAA. (In other words, the greatest common divisor of all elements of AAA should be 111.)
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string "YES" if it is possible to make the GCD of all elements of A equal to 1 or "NO" if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, we can divide 10 by 2, 15 by 3, and 30 by 2 to make the GCD of all elements equal to 1. For the second test case, we cannot make the GCD of all elements equal to 1 because 5 and 10 do not have any common divisors less than or equal to 4.
We can divide 4 by 2 and 6 by 3 to make the GCD of all elements equal to 1.
We cannot make the GCD of all elements equal to 1 because 2 does not have any divisors less than or equal to 1.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to check if it is possible to make the GCD of all elements of A equal to 1 by dividing each element by its divisors. We can use the concept of prime factorization to find the divisors of each element. If we can find a divisor for each element that is less than or equal to K, then we can make the GCD of all elements equal to 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * sqrt(max(Ai))) — explain: per query is O(N * sqrt(max(Ai))), total is O(T * N * sqrt(max(Ai)))
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain: we need to store the elements of A
